title: Xtra Peppery Piedmontese Paneer Piadina
description: Peppery, gooey, nostalgic Italian panbread with paneer that is double-fried for a crunchy, crispy finish.
instructions: Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Add warm water gradually while stirring until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, then cover and rest 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into four equal portions, roll each into a 12 cm circle.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a nonstick pan over medium heat.
Fry each dough circle until golden on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove the fried dough and set aside on a paper towel.
In the same pan, add remaining oil and a teaspoon of pepper flakes, then scatter paneer cubes over the dough.
Fry the paneer-topped dough until the paneer melts and the edges become crisp, about 3 minutes.
Flip once, cook until golden, then remove and garnish with fresh basil before serving hot.
